binaries.title: cmimcext.sys - ETW EventDataDescriptor size miscalculation causes pool overread

changed_functions.name: CmCompleteInitMachineConfig
key_changes: NEW: call to EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c)() at the top of the linked-list iteration loop (0x1c0007068 patched). EvaluateCurrentState is new in the patched build (inserted at 0x1c000166c, shifting later functions such as rbc_InitializeFeatureStaging down to 0x1c0001698). It is a feature-staging gate: it calls EvaluateFeature on Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757 and returns non-zero when that servicing fix is enabled.

NEW: When EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c)() returns non-zero, the code halves (>> 1, 'shr cx,1' / 'shr r9w,1') the node's +0x18 (key-name Length) and +0x28 (value-name Length) fields BEFORE passing them to McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0). This corrects for the fact that McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) internally doubles the values (add eax,eax at 0x1c00012e8) to convert char-counts to byte-counts.

NEW: When EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c)() returns 0 (original path), the raw byte-lengths are passed directly — these get doubled again inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0), producing 2x oversized EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR sizes. This retained legacy path makes the change a staged rollout rather than an unconditional fix.

The patched build adds the feature-descriptor global g_Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757_FeatureDescriptorDetails (referenced by EvaluateCurrentState at 0x1c0001670/0x1c000167c), which is absent from the unpatched build.
changed_functions.security_notes: The nodes in the linked list at data_1c00030b0 (CmpImcAllowEventQueueHead) store UNICODE_STRING key/value name pairs populated by ImpAddAllowListEvent (sub_1c00098a8). UNICODE_STRING.Length is in BYTES; each name buffer is allocated to MaximumLength bytes and holds Length valid bytes (RtlCopyUnicodeString). McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) is a generated ETW template helper that treats its length arguments as CHARACTER counts and unconditionally doubles them (add eax,eax at 0x1c00012e8/0x1c0001309) to produce byte sizes for EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR. The unpatched build passes the raw byte-lengths, so the doubling yields a 2x oversized descriptor. The patched build gates the fix behind a feature-staging check: EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c) returns non-zero only when Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757 is enabled, in which case the lengths are pre-halved (shr) so the doubling reproduces the correct byte count; when the feature is not enabled it returns 0 and the legacy raw-byte path is still taken. In the vulnerable path EtwWriteTransfer reads the 2x descriptor size, over-reading up to Length bytes past the valid data (out-of-bounds pool read: kernel information disclosure, potential BSOD if it crosses a page boundary).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED vulnerable path (no EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c) check, raw byte-lengths passed directly):
0x1c0007065 : 40387b10          cmp     byte [rbx+0x10], dil    ; check flag
0x1c0007069 : 7412              je      0x1c000707d
0x1c000706b : f605b6c0ffff02    test    byte [rel 0x1c0003128], 0x2
0x1c0007072 : 743e              je      0x1c00070b2
0x1c0007074 : 488d1595b1ffff    lea     rdx, [rel 0x1c0002210]  ; event template
0x1c000707b : eb10              jmp     0x1c000708d
...
0x1c000708d : 488b4330          mov     rax, qword [rbx+0x30]  ; value name buffer
0x1c0007091 : 440fb74b18        movzx   r9d, word [rbx+0x18]   ; key-name Length IN BYTES (NOT halved!)
0x1c0007096 : 4889442430        mov     qword [rsp+0x30], rax
0x1c000709b : 0fb74328          movzx   eax, word [rbx+0x28]   ; value-name Length IN BYTES (NOT halved!)
0x1c000709f : 6689442428        mov     word [rsp+0x28], ax
0x1c00070a4 : 488b4320          mov     rax, qword [rbx+0x20]  ; key name buffer
0x1c00070a8 : 4889442420        mov     qword [rsp+0x20], rax
0x1c00070ad : e8eea1ffff        call    0x1c00012a0            ; McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) DOUBLES these again

INSIDE McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) (byte-identical in both binaries — this is the sink):
0x1c00012e0 : movzx   eax, r9w                 ; arg4 = key-name length
0x1c00012e8 : add     eax, eax                 ; DOUBLES the byte-length
0x1c00012f2 : mov     [rbp+3Fh+var_38], eax    ; EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.Size = 2x bytes
0x1c0001305 : movzx   eax, [rbp+3Fh+arg_28]    ; value-name length
0x1c0001309 : add     eax, eax                 ; DOUBLES the byte-length
0x1c000130f : mov     [rbp+3Fh+var_18], eax    ; EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.Size = 2x bytes

PATCHED safe path (EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c) check + halving):
0x1c0007068 : e8ffa5ffff        call    0x1c000166c           ; NEW: feature-staging gate (IMCMemoryCorruptionFix)
0x1c000706d : 8a4b10            mov     cl, byte [rbx+0x10]   ; check flag
0x1c0007070 : 85c0              test    eax, eax              ; EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c) returned non-zero?
0x1c0007072 : 7461              je      0x1c00070d5           ; if 0, take old vulnerable path
...
0x1c0007085 : 0fb74b28          movzx   ecx, word [rbx+0x28]  ; value-name Length (bytes)
0x1c0007089 : 488b4330          mov     rax, qword [rbx+0x30] ; value-name buffer
0x1c000708d : 440fb74b18        movzx   r9d, word [rbx+0x18]  ; key-name Length (bytes)
0x1c0007092 : 66d1e9            shr     cx, 0x1               ; HALVE value-name length (NEW!)
0x1c0007095 : 4889442430        mov     qword [rsp+0x30], rax
0x1c000709a : 6641d1e9          shr     r9w, 0x1              ; HALVE key-name length (NEW!)
0x1c000709e : 66894c2428        mov     word [rsp+0x28], cx
0x1c00070a3 : lea     rdx, IMC_ETW_EVENT_IMC_VALUE_SET
0x1c00070aa : jmp     0x1c0007122
0x1c0007122 : mov     rax, [rbx+0x20]           ; key-name buffer
0x1c0007126 : mov     [rsp+0x20], rax
0x1c000712b : call    McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2      ; doubles the HALVED values, producing correct byte counts

security_findings.entry_point: CmSetInitMachineConfig (exported) -> populates linked list; CmCompleteInitMachineConfig (exported) -> processes and logs via ETW
call_chain: CmSetInitMachineConfig (exported, 0x1c0009010) — populates data_1c00030b0 linked list via ImpApplyMachineConfiguration (sub_1c0007428)/ImpAddAllowListEvent (sub_1c00098a8)
ImpAddAllowListEvent (sub_1c00098a8) (0x1c00098a8) — allocates 0x38-byte nodes, copies UNICODE_STRING key/value names (Length in bytes) into node+0x18 and node+0x28
CmCompleteInitMachineConfig (exported, 0x1c0007030) — iterates linked list, passes node+0x18 and node+0x28 as lengths to McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0)
McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) (0x1c00012a0) — builds EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR with cbSize = arg4*2 and arg_30*2 (DOUBLES byte-lengths)
McGenEventWrite (sub_1c000111c) (0x1c000111c) — calls EtwWriteTransfer with oversized data descriptors
EtwWriteTransfer — reads 2*Length bytes from each PagedPool buffer; when 2*Length exceeds the buffer MaximumLength it reads up to Length bytes past the allocation
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Out-of-bounds read / information disclosure (pool overread via miscalculated EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR size)
security_findings.description: CmCompleteInitMachineConfig iterates a linked list of machine configuration entries. Each node (allocated by ImpAddAllowListEvent (sub_1c00098a8) as 0x38 bytes in PagedPool) contains two UNICODE_STRING structures: key-name at +0x18 (Length in bytes) with buffer at +0x20, and value-name at +0x28 (Length in bytes) with buffer at +0x30. Each buffer is allocated to UNICODE_STRING.MaximumLength bytes via ExAllocatePoolWithTag (the NumberOfBytes argument is the MaximumLength field, read from [r14+2]/[rsi+2] in ImpAddAllowListEvent). When logging each entry via ETW, CmCompleteInitMachineConfig passes the raw byte-lengths to McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0). Inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0), these values are treated as character counts and unconditionally doubled ('add eax,eax' at 0x1c00012e8 and 0x1c0001309) to compute EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.Size. Since the values are ALREADY in bytes, the doubling produces a Size that is 2x the valid byte length. EtwWriteTransfer then reads Size bytes from each PagedPool buffer; when 2*Length exceeds MaximumLength (the common case for registry-derived names where MaximumLength is close to Length), it reads up to Length bytes past the end of the allocation. The patch introduces a check via EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c)(); when it returns non-zero (feature Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757 enabled), the byte-lengths are pre-halved (shr cx,1 / shr r9w,1) so that the subsequent doubling in McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) yields the correct byte count. When the feature is not enabled the original unhalved path is retained, so this is a staged servicing rollout rather than an unconditional fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. CmSetInitMachineConfig must be called first to populate the data_1c00030b0 linked list with at least one node. Nodes are created by enumerating registry keys under \REGISTRY\MACHINE\INITIALMACHINECONFIG and related paths. 2. CmCompleteInitMachineConfig must be called (it is an exported function of cmimcext.sys). 3. The linked list must contain entries where data_1c0003128 (Microsoft_Windows_CoreSystem_InitMachineConfigEnableBits) has bit 1 or bit 2 set (ETW logging enabled). 4. On the unpatched binary there is no feature gate in the loop, so the raw byte-lengths are always passed directly and the overread occurs for every non-empty node. On the patched binary the overread still occurs whenever the servicing feature Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757 is not enabled (EvaluateCurrentState returns 0). The overread per buffer is up to the buffer's Length in bytes (e.g., a 20-byte valid name yields a 40-byte ETW descriptor, over-reading up to 20 bytes past the valid data).
security_findings.primitive: Bounded kernel pool over-read (information disclosure). EtwWriteTransfer reads 2*Length bytes from a name buffer allocated at MaximumLength; when 2*Length exceeds MaximumLength it reads up to Length bytes of adjacent PagedPool into the ETW event payload. The over-read is bounded by the name's own valid length and is read-only; it is not attacker-scalable beyond that. Reachability is limited: CmSetInitMachineConfig/CmCompleteInitMachineConfig are boot-time initialization exports (not IOCTL handlers), the name lengths come from the admin/setup-controlled \REGISTRY\MACHINE\INITIALMACHINECONFIG hive, and the disclosed bytes are only visible to an administrator-level consumer of the Microsoft-Windows-CoreSystem-InitMachineConfig ETW session. No unprivileged privilege boundary is crossed, and there is no demonstrable path to arbitrary read/write, KASLR defeat, or code execution. If a name buffer sits at the end of a special-pool page, the doubled read can instead fault at the guard page.

SINK: McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 @ 0x1c00012a0 (byte-identical in both binaries):
0x1c00012e0 : movzx   eax, r9w                 ; arg4 = key-name length (bytes in unpatched)
0x1c00012e8 : add     eax, eax                 ; DOUBLES: 20-byte length -> Size=40
0x1c00012f2 : mov     [rbp+3Fh+var_38], eax     ; EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.Size
0x1c0001305 : movzx   eax, [rbp+3Fh+arg_28]     ; value-name length (bytes in unpatched)
0x1c0001309 : add     eax, eax                 ; DOUBLES: 20-byte length -> Size=40
0x1c000130f : mov     [rbp+3Fh+var_18], eax     ; EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.Size
0x1c000131b : call    McGenEventWrite          ; -> EtwWriteTransfer (reads Size bytes from buffer)

breakpoints.why: Entry point of the vulnerable function. Verify the linked list at data_1c00030b0 is non-empty (rbx will be loaded from it at 0x1c000705c). Step to the loop body at 0x1c0007065 to reach the vulnerable call path.

The ETW logging sink where byte-lengths are doubled. At entry, r9w (arg4) holds the key-name Length in bytes, and the value-name Length in bytes is read from the stack frame slot arg_28. Watch the 'add eax,eax' at 0x1c00012e8 to see the doubling. Compare the resulting Size (2*Length) against the buffer's MaximumLength allocation at [rsp+0x20] / arg5.

The call to McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) with unhalved byte-lengths. At this point: r9w = node+0x18 (key-name Length in bytes), [rsp+0x28] = node+0x28 (value-name Length in bytes), [rsp+0x20] = node+0x20 (key-name buffer ptr), [rsp+0x30] = node+0x30 (value-name buffer ptr). Verify r9w equals the actual buffer allocation size — it does, and McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) will double it.

watch: r9w at 0x1c0007091: holds key-name UNICODE_STRING.Length in BYTES (e.g., 0x28 = 40 bytes). This is the value that will be doubled inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0).
word [rbx+0x28] at 0x1c000709b: holds value-name UNICODE_STRING.Length in BYTES. Also doubled inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0).

eax at 0x1c00012e8 (inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0)): the result of 'add eax,eax' — this is the oversized EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.cbSize. For a 40-byte buffer, eax will be 0x50 (80), reading 40 bytes past the allocation.
[rbx+0x20] and [rbx+0x30]: key-name and value-name buffer pointers. Use !pool to check their actual allocation sizes and compare against the doubled cbSize.
key_offsets: 0x1c0007065: start of linked-list loop body (cmp byte [rbx+0x10], dil — flag check)

0x1c000708d-0x1c00070ad: VULNERABLE call setup — reads node+0x18/+0x28 as raw byte-lengths, passes to McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) WITHOUT halving. In the PATCHED binary, this same block is gated behind 'call 0x1c000166c' at 0x1c0007068 and 'shr cx,1'/'shr r9w,1' halving at 0x1c0007085/0x1c0007092.
0x1c00012e8: 'add eax,eax' — the doubling instruction inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0). This is the root cause sink, unchanged in both binaries.
0x1c0001309: second 'add eax,eax' — doubles the value-name length inside McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0).
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: CmCompleteInitMachineConfig and CmSetInitMachineConfig are exported functions of cmimcext.sys, a kernel-mode DLL loaded during system initialization. The linked list is populated by CmSetInitMachineConfig, which reads from registry keys under \REGISTRY\MACHINE\INITIALMACHINECONFIG. To trigger: (1) Ensure the registry hive at \REGISTRY\MACHINE\INITIALMACHINECONFIG contains key/value name entries with non-empty UNICODE_STRING names (this happens during normal OS setup/initialization). (2) Call CmSetInitMachineConfig (populates the list). (3) Call CmCompleteInitMachineConfig (iterates and logs — this is where the overread occurs). The ETW logging flags (data_1c0003128 bits 1 and 2) must be set. In the unpatched binary, there is no halving — every entry triggers the overread.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Enable ETW tracing for the provider 'Microsoft-Windows-CoreSystem-InitMachineConfig' (GUID at data_1c0002258). When CmCompleteInitMachineConfig processes the linked list, each ETW event describes 2*Length bytes per name; where 2*Length exceeds the buffer MaximumLength, the tail of that data is PagedPool bytes past the name buffer's allocation. If Special Pool is enabled for cmimcext.sys (tag 'IMCP' / 0x50434d49) and a name buffer is placed at the end of a special-pool page, the doubled read reaches the guard page and faults, producing a page-fault bugcheck at the copy (the exact bugcheck code is whatever the memory manager raises for a read beyond a special-pool allocation; it is not asserted here). Without Special Pool the over-read silently places adjacent pool bytes into the ETW event payload.
debugging_aids.notes: The node structure (0x38 bytes, PagedPool, tag 0x50434d49) layout is: +0x00 Flink, +0x08 Blink, +0x10 flag byte, +0x18 key-name UNICODE_STRING.Length (2 bytes), +0x1a key-name UNICODE_STRING.MaximumLength (2 bytes), +0x20 key-name UNICODE_STRING.Buffer (8 bytes), +0x28 value-name UNICODE_STRING.Length (2 bytes), +0x2a value-name UNICODE_STRING.MaximumLength (2 bytes), +0x30 value-name UNICODE_STRING.Buffer (8 bytes). The key-name and value-name buffers are separately allocated to UNICODE_STRING.MaximumLength bytes via ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, MaximumLength, 0x50434d49), each holding Length valid bytes. The 2x descriptor over-reads up to Length bytes past the valid data. The patched binary's EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c)() is a feature-staging gate that calls EvaluateFeature on Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757 and returns non-zero only when that servicing fix is enabled; when it returns 0 (feature not enabled) the legacy raw-byte path is taken. EvaluateCurrentState is new in the patched build (inserted at 0x1c000166c), which relocates rbc_InitializeFeatureStaging from 0x1c000166c to 0x1c0001698. The patched build also adds the feature-descriptor global g_Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757_FeatureDescriptorDetails, absent from the unpatched build.

summary: The patch corrects an out-of-bounds pool read (CWE-125) in cmimcext.sys's CmCompleteInitMachineConfig. The function iterates a linked list of machine configuration entries whose nodes store UNICODE_STRING key/value name pairs with Length fields measured in bytes; each name buffer is allocated at MaximumLength. When logging these entries via ETW (McTemplateK0hzr0hzr2 (sub_1c00012a0) → McGenEventWrite → EtwWriteTransfer), the byte-identical logging helper doubles the length arguments (treating them as character counts), producing EVENT_DATA_DESCRIPTOR.Size values of 2*Length. When 2*Length exceeds MaximumLength, EtwWriteTransfer reads up to Length bytes past the buffer into the ETW payload. The corrective code is a call to EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c000166c)() that, when the servicing feature Feature_Servicing_IMCMemoryCorruptionFix_61262757 is enabled, pre-halves the byte-lengths so the doubling reproduces correct byte counts; when the feature is not enabled the original over-reading path is retained, making this a staged servicing rollout rather than an unconditional fix. Severity is Low: the over-read is bounded by the name's own length and read-only, it is reached only through boot-time initialization exports over the admin/setup-controlled INITIALMACHINECONFIG hive, and the disclosed bytes are visible only to an administrator-level consumer of the Microsoft-Windows-CoreSystem-InitMachineConfig ETW session. No unprivileged privilege boundary is crossed, and no arbitrary read/write, KASLR defeat, or code-execution path is demonstrable.
